responseMessageContents.text:
## Goal
- Build and deploy enterprise time zone architecture, fix owner sign-in, align colors with IVX landing palette, QA/redesign Reels card, fix chat UX issues, redesign IVX registration/login experience, add required birthday + gender fields, run 30-root-cause Expo Go forensic repair, execute complete 20-section landing page QA, complete final landing page closeout (CORS, CLS, accessibility, scroll restoration, owner recovery auth, 404, canonical redirect, bundle audit), and repair Expo Go "Failed to download remote update" device failure

## Constraints & Preferences
- Store ALL timestamps in UTC only; convert to local only for display
- Owner account must NEVER sign in automatically — manual email + password required on every launch
- App colors must match landing page — `#FFD700` gold, `#00C48C` green, `#FF4D4D` red, `#4A90D9` blue
- Do not change Android package name (`com.ivxholdings.app`) or iOS bundle identifier
- Do not create new project, replace IVX, reset repo, switch to localhost, or add Rork dependencies
- Birthday field REQUIRED (MM/DD/YYYY, age 18-120, stored as `dateOfBirth` ISO YYYY-MM-DD)
- Gender field REQUIRED — chip selector: Male / Female / Prefer not to say
- Landing QA: do not mark production ready because routes return 200; require live evidence
- Expo Go: do not create new project, do not run create-expo-app, do not remove production modules
- CORS must allow only: `https://ivxholding.com`, `https://chat.ivxholding.com`, approved local dev origins
- GitHub pushes require orphan branch technique to exclude `.rork/history/` files with secrets
- Do not reuse previous `exp.direct` tunnel URLs — must generate new URL after invalidation
- Do not say manifest 200 is sufficient — must verify bundle + assets + content types
- Use `bunx` not `npx` (npx not allowed in sandbox)

## Progress
### Done
- **Owner auto-login removal FULLY DEPLOYED AND VERIFIED**: Commits `c67f8a2`, `e831c8d`, `e936eef`; 3 auto-login paths blocked in `auth-context.tsx`
- **Owner sign-in fix FULLY DEPLOYED — USER CONFIRMED WORKING**: Commit `d6dbfce` → GitHub `43be880c`
- **App color alignment DEPLOYED**: 784 hardcoded colors replaced across 104 Expo files
- **Enterprise Time Zone System DEPLOYED**: 69/69 tests pass, 17/17 API endpoints verified, 8 cities + DST verified
- **Reels card redesigned DEPLOYED**: commit `730aa4d`
- **Chat UX fixes DEPLOYED AND E2E VERIFIED**: commit `c0470a3`, 5/5 messages OK
- **IVX Registration Redesign DEPLOYED**: `IVXAuthCard.tsx` (~1900 lines), `expo/app/auth.tsx`
- **Birthday field DEPLOYED**: Backend validation 4/4 scenarios pass
- **Gender field DEPLOYED**: `GENDER_OPTIONS` chip selector, `VALID_GENDERS` Set in backend
- **Landing page 20-section QA completed — fixes deployed and verified**
- **Final closeout commit `3abaa17` DEPLOYED AND VERIFIED LIVE on Render**:
  - Backend SHA `3abaa17f` matches GitHub `3abaa17` (boot `2026-07-13T01:07:23.342Z`)
  - CORS: `access-control-allow-origin: https://ivxholding.com` (not `*`) — verified live on `/api/ivx/properties/featured`
  - Owner recovery: `/api/ivx/owner-recovery/status` without auth → 401 — verified live
  - All routes 200: `/health`, `/api/ivx/properties/featured`, `/api/ivx/jv-deals`, `/api/landing-config`, `/api/ivx/analytics`
  - Protected routes: `/api/ivx/members` without auth → 401
  - Rate limiting: 5 attempts → 401, 6th → 429
  - Signup E2E: `userId=e39c1586-5560-4887-b707-f9512afa2a83` created successfully
  - Validation: missing gender → 400 "Gender is required.", owner role → 400 "Please select at least one role to continue."
  - Landing HTML CLS fix, scroll restoration, keyboard nav, accessibility all in committed code
- **Final closeout report compiled: 9 PASS / 6 FAIL** (6 failures require physical device or DNS/Cloudflare access)
- **Expo Go repair Steps 1-4 completed**:
  - Step 1: Old tunnel `exp://9gmyh1o-anonymous-8081.exp.direct` INVALIDATED, all Metro/ngrok processes killed, ports 8081/8082/8083 freed, caches cleared
  - Step 2: Failure classified as TUNNEL/NETWORK FAILURE — phone was trying to reconnect to dead tunnel URL, not an IVX app crash
  - Step 3: SDK 54.0.35, RN 0.81.5, expo-router 6.0.24, React 19.1.0, expo-updates NOT INSTALLED, `updates.enabled: false` — compatible with Expo Go 2.33.x+
  - Step 4: No expo-updates conflict — `updates.url` not set, no `runtimeVersion`, `channel`, or `projectId` configured
- **Expo Go Step 6 (bundle optimization) partially done**:
  - Deferred owner AI watchdog (`ivxAIWatchdog`), incident capture, text-node-guard from startup to 3s after boot in `app/_layout.tsx`
  - Fixed `metro.config.js` `useWatchman` typing warning with `@ts-expect-error`
- **Expo Go root cause IDENTIFIED**: Metro returns HTML (`text/html`) instead of JSON (`application/expo+json`) for ALL manifest requests — Expo Go can't parse manifest → throws `java.io.IOException: Failed to download remote update`
- **Fix attempted**: Removed `web.output: 'single'` from `app.config.ts` (was causing Metro to serve SPA HTML shell for all paths including `/manifest.android.json`) — but manifest STILL returns HTML after restart, investigation ongoing
- **New tunnel sessions created**: `exp://jyiupoq-anonymous-8081.exp.direct` (killed), `exp://eb3lc6s-anonymous-8081.exp.direct` (latest, Metro died during investigation)
- **Bundle verified through tunnel**: 39,101,052 bytes (37.3MB), HTTP 200, `application/javascript` content type
- **Assets verified as 404 through tunnel**: `icon.png`, `splash-icon.png`, `adaptive-icon.png`, `favicon.png` all return 404

### In Progress
- **Expo Go manifest HTML→JSON fix** — removed `web.output: 'single'` but manifest still returns HTML. Investigating Expo CLI manifest middleware: `ExpoGoManifestHandlerMiddleware.js` and `ManifestMiddleware.js` in `node_modules/@expo/cli/build/src/start/server/middleware/`. Metro process died during investigation, needs restart.
- **Need to determine why manifest middleware isn't intercepting requests** — possibly the expo-router web middleware is taking priority, or the Accept header detection isn't working

### Blocked
- **www.ivxholding.com does not 301 redirect to ivxholding.com** — requires Cloudflare DNS/page rule change
- **404 returns 200 on ivxholding.com** — S3 SPA fallback serves index.html for all paths; requires CloudFront Lambda edge function
- **Landing HTML not redeployed to S3** — AWS credentials not available in cloud sandbox (`AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID` not set)
- **Device QA (Android, iPhone, desktop) NOT VERIFIED** — requires physical devices
- **Media QA (play/pause/mute/swipe/like/comment) NOT VERIFIED** — requires real device
- **CSP HTTP header not applied on S3** — S3 serves static HTML without security headers
- **Bundle size 37.3MB not reducible below 15MB** — Expo Go uses dev mode; SDK 54 core + RN 0.81 + React 19 + 242 routes = ~37MB minimum

## Key Decisions
- CORS allowlist: `IVX_ALLOWED_ORIGINS` array in `hono.ts` with `https://ivxholding.com`, `https://www.ivxholding.com`, `https://chat.ivxholding.com`, `http://localhost:8081/3000/5173`, `http://127.0.0.1:8081/3000` — Hono `cors()` origin function returns `null` for non-allowlisted origins
- All 32 backend API files had `'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*'` replaced with `'https://ivxholding.com'` via `sed` batch fix
- Owner recovery auth: checks `readBearerToken(request)` against `process.env.IVX_OWNER_TOKEN` — returns 401 if missing/mismatched
- Fake watchman binary (`/tmp/fake-bin/watchman`) returns synthetic JSON for `get-sockname`, `watch-project`, `version` commands — unblocks Metro cold builds in cloud sandbox with `nice_value=19`
- Metro config blocklist: `SERVER_ONLY_PACKAGES` array of 48 packages blocked via `config.resolver.resolveRequest` — had no effect because packages were never imported in app source
- Orphan branch push technique: `git checkout --orphan` → `git add` specific files → `git commit` → `git push github HEAD:main --force`
- Deferred owner AI modules from startup: `setTimeout(() => { require(...) }, 3000)` in `_layout.tsx` useEffect — prevents 213KB+ owner-only code from blocking initial bundle
- Root cause of Expo Go failure: Metro's manifest endpoint returns HTML instead of JSON — Expo Go expects `application/expo+json` content type but gets `text/html` with SPA shell
- Expo CLI manifest middleware files: `ExpoGoManifestHandlerMiddleware.js` uses `accepts` package to negotiate content type based on `Accept` header — needs further investigation to determine why it's not activating

## Next Steps
- Investigate why Expo CLI manifest middleware (`ExpoGoManifestHandlerMiddleware.js`) isn't serving JSON manifests — may need to check middleware registration order or expo-router web middleware interference
- Restart Metro with fresh tunnel after manifest fix is confirmed
- Verify manifest returns JSON (`application/expo+json`) with proper Accept headers
- Verify all startup assets (icon, splash, adaptive-icon, favicon) return 200 through tunnel
- Keep tunnel alive for 10+ minutes and verify stability
- Provide owner phone cleanup instructions (Step 9): update Expo Go, clear cache, disable VPN/Private DNS
- Watch live device connection (Step 10) when owner opens new URL
- If tunnel fails twice after all fixes, build Android development APK via EAS (Step 13)
- Redeploy landing HTML to S3 (requires AWS credentials)
- Verify `www.ivxholding.com` 301 redirect (requires Cloudflare DNS change)

## Critical Context
- Backend deployed and verified: SHA `3abaa17f`, boot `2026-07-13T01:07:23.342Z` — all fixes live
- Git branch: `deploy-final-closeout`, SHA `e2e1c3b` (latest local commit after _layout.tsx and app.config.ts changes)
- Old tunnel `exp://9gmyh1o-anonymous-8081.exp.direct` is DEAD — must not be reused
- Latest tunnel was `exp://eb3lc6s-anonymous-8081.exp.direct` but Metro died — needs fresh restart
- `.expo/settings.json` was deleted to force new tunnel URL randomness
- `app.config.ts` `web.output: 'single'` removed but manifest still returns HTML — fix incomplete
- Manifest middleware exists at `node_modules/@expo/cli/build/src/start/server/middleware/ExpoGoManifestHandlerMiddleware.js` — uses `accepts` package for content negotiation
- `node_modules/@expo/cli/build/src/start/server/middleware/ManifestMiddleware.js` — base manifest handler
- Bundle entry point: `expo-router/entry` (not `index`) — `/index.bundle` returns 404, correct path is `/node_modules/expo-router/entry.bundle`
- Expo SDK 54.0.35 installed, CLI 54.0.25, expo-router 6.0.24, React 19.1.0, RN 0.81.5
- 242 route files, 130 heavy admin/owner/developer routes, 3,184 total import statements
- `src/modules/ivx-owner-ai/services/ivxAIRequestService.ts` is 212KB — largest source file

## Relevant Files
- `backend/hono.ts`: Main backend entry — CORS allowlist (`IVX_ALLOWED_ORIGINS`), rate limiting, all route registrations, health endpoint
- `backend/api/ivx-owner-recovery-sms.ts`: Owner recovery — requires bearer token for status endpoint
- `backend/api/*.ts`: 34 API files — all CORS wildcards replaced with `'https://ivxholding.com'`
- `expo/ivxholding-landing/index.html`: 8,978 lines — landing page with scroll restoration, keyboard nav, accessibility, CLS fixes
- `expo/metro.config.js`: Metro config with Watchman fallback (`useWatchman: false`), server-only package blocklist, `@ts-expect-error` for typing
- `expo/app.config.ts`: Expo config — `web.output: 'single'` REMOVED (was causing manifest HTML issue), `updates.enabled: false`, SDK 54
- `expo/app/_layout.tsx`: Root layout — 5 startup providers, owner AI modules deferred to 3s after boot
- `expo/scripts/deploy-landing-index-only.mjs`: S3 + CloudFront deploy script (requires AWS credentials)
- `expo/lib/auth-context.tsx`: Auth context — 3 auto-login guard paths blocked
- `expo/components/IVXAuthCard.tsx`: Registration/login UI — ~1900 lines, glass card design
- `expo/node_modules/@expo/cli/build/src/start/server/middleware/ExpoGoManifestHandlerMiddleware.js`: Manifest handler — uses `accepts` for content negotiation, returns JSON for Expo Go
- `expo/node_modules/@expo/cli/build/src/start/server/middleware/ManifestMiddleware.js`: Base manifest middleware
- `/tmp/fake-bin/watchman`: Fake watchman binary — unblocks Metro in cloud sandbox (not committed)
